areasOfConcern: 18.2% cited supervisor relationship issues as primary exit reason
Multiple reports of racial inequality concerns
Career advancement limitations cited by respondents
positiveFeedback: 72.7% would still recommend Creighton as workplace
Strong university culture and mission alignment
Meaningful work with students highly valued

actionItems.text: Investigate reported improper conduct (3 of 11 respondents)
Implement supervisor training program - cited by 18% as exit reason
Improve response rate in Arts & Sciences (0 of 11 exits responded)
Enhance career development programs - limited advancement cited
Build on improved response rate (17.74% vs typical 10-15%)

summaryText:
With a 17.74% response rate (11 of 62 exits), the exit survey provides meaningful insights, though increased participation would strengthen data reliability. 72.7% of respondents would still recommend Creighton as a workplace. Key improvement areas include supervisor relationships, career advancement opportunities, and addressing reported improper conduct.

Notable patterns: Athletics and Medicine/Dentistry show higher response rates (40-50%), while larger departments like Arts & Sciences show low participation. Focus on improving response rates in underrepresented departments for comprehensive retention insights.
